chapter_goal: Explain DH density as pushforward symplectic volume and as reduced-space volume variation.

source_span_read.title: Duistermaat-Heckman Theorems
source_span_read.source_note: Read the local PDF text for Liouville measure, DH pushforward measure, Radon-Nikodym density, the S^2 area example, local reduced-space form, linear variation of reduced cohomology classes, polynomial volume proof, and Homework 23 on S^1-equivariant cohomology and localization.

library_routing.representation: height-band area and constant density plot
library_routing.why: the source example is a level-band area computation

library_routing.representation: sigma block matrix and proof dependency graph
library_routing.why: nondegeneracy and the moment-map contraction are algebraic checks

library_routing.concept: linear cohomology class and polynomial volume
library_routing.why: the theorem follows by expanding (omega_red - t beta)^(n-1)
toric manifolds have constant DH density over the moment polytope

library_routing.representation: Cartan differential route and exact checks
library_routing.why: the exercise is a complex of identities rather than a geometric plot

visual_sequence.inspection_target: see that equal height bands have area 2*pi times vertical length
trace sigma, connection curvature, and moment projection x
visual_sequence.validation: symbolic sigma matrix is nondegenerate at x=0 and i_X sigma = dx
SymPy expansion has degree n-1 and matches sampled values

visual_sequence.inspection_target: connect moment polytope area with symplectic volume
visual_sequence.validation: volume=(2*pi)^2 area for a triangle model

visual_sequence.concept: Cartan differential and equivariant symplectic form
visual_sequence.inspection_target: see why d_S1^2 vanishes on invariant forms and why d_S1(omega+mu x)=0 is the moment-map equation
